JOURNAL (current thesis state):
# Journal — Investing Agent

## 1. Market view

Universe broadly weak heading into the July earnings cluster; cash at 66.4% reflects patience rather than conviction. Seven positions across five sectors, all starter-to-moderate size. The next 40 days are the critical adjudication window: ATRLJ ~Jul 10, CORE-B ~Jul 22, VOLV/ESSITY ~Jul 24, RUSTA Q1 TBD, ASSA/THULE ~Jul 28–29. No urgency to add before those prints resolve. Rusta initiated this week as the cleanest new signal; ASSA-B deferred for a better entry post-Q2.

## 2. Holdings

**HM-B.ST** — Core | Consumer Discretionary | 4.9%
- **Thesis:** Karl-Johan Persson bought ~1.2 BSEK across six transactions in eight days into an 11% drawdown — one of the strongest insider signals possible on Swedish Large Cap. Starter position pending fundamental confirmation.
- **Sell if:** Profit warning, gross margin collapse, or confirmed structural market-share loss to Shein/Temu. Not on price weakness alone.
- **Status:** `intact`

**ESSITY-B.ST** — Core | Consumer Staples | 8.0%
- **Thesis:** Exceptional 90-day insider cluster — 512 MSEK, 7 buyers, 0 sellers — led by Industrivärden. Defensive staples compounder at ~14x P/E with 3.4% yield. Q2 ~Jul 24 is the next adjudicator.
- **Sell if:** Sustained margin deterioration where input costs structurally exceed pricing power, or Q2 shows revenue decline accelerating with margin compression.
- **Status:** `intact`

**THULE.ST** — Core | Consumer Discretionary | 3.5%
- **Thesis:** Ten insiders including chair, vice chair, CEO, and CFO bought ~14–25 MSEK across two waves. Quality mid-cap franchise 28% off highs; weakness is cyclical not structural. Q2 ~Jul 29 is the next adjudicator.
- **Sell if:** Insider turns net seller, operating margin compresses below ~13%, or Q2 reveals genuine earnings deterioration rather than cyclical softness.
- **Status:** `intact`

**VOLV-B.ST** — Core | Industrials | 4.8%
- **Thesis:** Industrivärden deployed ~290 MSEK in a single day near 52-week highs. Net cash ~58 BSEK, ~4% yield, 9.8% operating margin. Controlling owner buying near highs endorses forward earnings. Q2 ~Jul 24.
- **Sell if:** Industrivärden becomes net seller, profit warning, or Q2 reveals order intake collapse rather than cyclical normalisation.
- **Status:** `intact`

**ATRLJ-B.ST** — Core | Real Estate | 4.8%
- **Thesis:** Tagehus (controlling family) bought ~30 MSEK into Varma's forced 7%-discount block exit. Ericsson 58k sqm Hagastaden anchor lease provides fundamental support. Starter size given net debt/equity 101%. Q2 ~Jul 10.
- **Sell if:** Further forced-seller overhang without insider response; Q2 reveals deteriorating occupancy/lease renewals; or Ericsson lease proves less economically material than signalled.
- **Status:** `intact`

**CORE-B.ST** — Aggressive | Real Estate | 2.9%
- **Thesis:** Rutger Arnhult bought ~110 MSEK personally across two waves into a 50%-off, capitulating leveraged commercial real estate name. FCF positive (678 MSEK R12); this is a leverage/rate story, not a going-concern story. Q2 ~Jul 22.
- **Sell if:** Dilutive equity raise, covenant breach, LTV deterioration in Q2, or Arnhult becomes a net seller.
- **Status:** `intact`

**RUSTA.ST** — Core | Consumer Discretionary | 4.5%
- **Thesis:** Controlling owner Aforber Invest bought ~52 MSEK across 13 transactions (including a single 30.6 MSEK ticket) days after a positive Q4 print and dividend raise, into a -23% drawdown. Discount retail has structural counter-cyclical tailwind. Starter size pending verification of the D/E 243% leverage (likely IFRS-16-driven but unconfirmed).
- **Sell if:** Leverage confirmed as genuine financial debt rather than operating leases and the deleveraging path is unclear; same-store sales deteriorate; or Aforber Invest becomes a net seller.
- **Status:** `intact`

## 3. Watchlist

**ASSA-B.ST** — Broad insider cluster (CEO ~35 MSEK + 4 operating managers) across April–June. Q2 Jul 28 is the gate. Target entry ≤340 SEK; Latour block-sale overhang may deliver it. Revisit post-print or on pullback below 335.

**VIMIAN.ST** — Fidelio Vet + CEO bought ~128 MSEK over two weeks; signal is real but anchor-holder concentrated and FCF is -243 MEUR. Q2 ~Jul 29. Analyst would-buy-at 27 SEK; watch into the print, reassess fundamentals then.

**NOLA-B.ST** — Principal owner Herenco bought ~25 MSEK in three clustered June purchases; clean signal endorsing the CEO transition. Revenue declining multi-year (-3.2% YoY). Entry target ~47 SEK; prefer to wait for Q2 (Aug 5) print under new CEO before sizing.

**LIFCO-B.ST** — Strong insider cluster (3 distinct buyers, board member 2x, ~10 MSEK around Q1 print) on a quality compounder. Price at 307 is above the 275–285 entry zone. Wait for Q2 (~Jul 24) pullback or post-print confirmation; re-screen week of Jul 21.

**HEXA-B.ST** — 4 management buyers including CFO bought ~12.4 MSEK into a -20% drawdown; no sellers. Data quality flag: fundamentals block contains implausible values (P/E 0.9, FCF yield 154%) — cannot validate valuation. Q2 ~Jul 25. Starter defensible on insider signal alone if data is cleaned up; otherwise wait for print.

## 4. Lessons learned / open questions

- **Rusta fill vs limit:** PM set limit at 86 SEK, execution filled at 75.50 — meaningfully better entry. No lesson to change, but note that the market offered a 12% better price than the limit; the conservative limit was correct given elevated vol (48.6%).
- **Consumer Discretionary concentration:** HM + Thule + Rusta = ~13% of portfolio. No further Consumer Discretionary adds without rebalancing one of these, even if the insider signal is strong. The sector cap (25%) is not close, but three correlated names is enough.
- **Correlated real estate:** ATRLJ + CORE-B = ~7.7%, both leveraged Swedish commercial real estate. No further real estate adds without rebalancing one first.
- **Open question — earnings cluster:** ATRLJ (Jul 10), CORE-B (Jul 22), VOLV/ESSITY (Jul 24), ASSA/THULE (Jul 28–29) all land in a 19-day window. Consider whether any pre-print trimming is warranted, or whether current starter sizing provides sufficient cushion. Reassess at next weekly cycle once ATRLJ reports first.

  - [WATCH] HM-B.ST (other): Börsvärlden reports speculation of imminent CEO change, Clas Ohlson CEO named as candidate — unconfirmed media; no price reaction today but thesis rests partly on KJ Persson insider conviction, so leadership change warrants reassessment at weekly cycle.
New-name insider signals (not currently held — you may open these):
  - SECU-B.ST: significant insider buying in last 2 days — see Analyst note below
  - SCST.ST: significant insider buying in last 2 days — see Analyst note below
  - AAK.ST: significant insider buying in last 2 days — see Analyst note below
Material news today (>=M3):
  - HM-B.ST: M4 Media speculation that H&M CEO change is imminent, with Clas Ohlson's CEO named as obvious successor candidate.

ANALYST NOTES on new (not-yet-held) names surfaced today:
# Securitas (SECU-B.ST) — Analyst Note

**Thesis.** Securitas is a large, cash-generative security services business trading on a reasonable ~16x P/E with a ~3.5% dividend yield. The standout signal this week is a broad insider cluster: the CEO, CFO, and 8+ other managers bought in mid-May and early June — the kind of multi-person buying pattern that historically carries weight on the Stockholm exchange. Offsetting this, Latour (long-term anchor owner) just sold ~5 BSEK worth of stock, and reported revenue is shrinking.

**Positives**
- Broad insider cluster: 10 distinct buyers including CEO Ahlqvist and CFO Dall'Ora, concentrated in two waves (May 12 and June 4). This is a meaningful conviction signal.
- Decent cash generation: FCF ~8.4 BSEK, FCF yield ~9.7%, supporting the dividend.
- Valuation undemanding for a defensive industrial: P/E 16, ROE 13%, 3.5% yield.
- Price is roughly flat vs 200-day MA and -12% off 52w high — not chasing a rally.

**Concerns**
- Revenue -6.5% YoY on R12 is a real negative — I can't tell from the data I have whether this is FX, divestitures, or genuine demand weakness.
- Latour selling 5 BSEK is a material overhang; a long-term anchor reducing exposure is the opposite signal to the management buying, and the market may need time to digest the supply.
- Net debt 32 BSEK / 77.5% of equity is on the higher side — not alarming for this kind of business, but limits flexibility.
- Operating margin 6.1% and net margin 3.6% are thin; the business has limited cushion if revenue keeps slipping.
- I cannot evaluate organic vs reported growth, segment trends, or Q1 commentary from the data I have — next earnings ~Aug 4 will clarify.

**Insider read.** Strong positive cluster — CEO + CFO + 8 other managers buying in coordinated waves is unusual and credible. The single sell by Helena Andreas is larger in SEK but is one person vs ten, and management-level buying is a stronger signal than a single sell. Net 90d is technically a small net sell only because of that one transaction; the *pattern* is bullish.

**Price read.** Price is sitting right at the 200-day MA (+0.4%), down 12% from 52w high, with modest 22% vol. Neither overbought nor capitulating — a reasonable accumulation zone if the thesis holds.

**Verdict.** The insider cluster is genuinely interesting and would normally pull me toward action, but the combination of Latour's 5 BSEK overhang, declining revenue, and earnings only 6 weeks away argues for patience. Better to watch through the August print than to step in front of a known seller.

**Verdict: WATCH — not actionable now, keep on watchlist**

If post-earnings the insider cluster still looks right and price is in the 135–145 zone, this becomes a more straightforward Core add."
```json
}
```

# Scandi Standard (SCST.ST) — Analyst Note

**Thesis.** A broad, sustained insider buying cluster — 9 distinct insiders, zero sellers, ~17.8 MSEK net bought over ~5 weeks, including the largest shareholder (Grupo Lusiaves) adding repeatedly and what appears to be senior management (Tunestål, Strömmen) writing meaningful personal cheques — is a strong signal on a Swedish mid-cap. The fundamentals are unremarkable (low-margin chicken processor), but the insider tape is the kind that usually precedes good news rather than bad.

**Positives**
- Insider cluster is unusually clean: 9 buyers / 0 sellers, multiple individuals, multiple dates, no equity-program noise. This is textbook "something good is coming" pattern on Small/Mid Cap.
- Strategic anchor (Grupo Lusiaves, a Portuguese poultry group and vice-chairman-linked entity) has been adding aggressively across June — that's an industry insider voting with cash, not financial speculation.
- Revenue growing +8.7% YoY on a 5.2% 3y CAGR — accelerating, not decelerating, in a defensive staples category.
- Stock has consolidated for 3 months (-0.1%) after a +44.7% 12m run, so insiders are buying into a base, not chasing.

**Concerns**
- Margins are structurally thin (operating 4.5%, net 2.8%) and FCF yield is only 1.5% — this is a commodity-ish business with limited pricing power and high input-cost sensitivity (feed, energy).
- Net debt / equity at 71.5% is meaningful for a low-margin business; any earnings hiccup hits the equity hard.
- P/E 23 on a 2.8% net margin business is not obviously cheap on absolute fundamentals — the buy case rests on the insider signal, not valuation.
- Earnings in ~36 days (est. 2026-07-28) — insiders are buying *into* a print, which is suggestive but also means buying now is implicitly trading the report.
- I don't have access to the Q1 report commentary, sell-side estimates, or news flow on avian flu / feed prices, so I cannot independently verify what the insiders may be seeing.

**Insider read.** Strong positive. The breadth (9 buyers), absence of any sellers, presence of the strategic shareholder, and clustering 5–6 weeks before earnings is among the more compelling insider patterns one sees on Swedish Mid Cap. This is the primary reason to be interested.

**Price read.** Px 141.80, +20% above 200MA, -14% from 52w high, 3-month flat. Consolidating after a strong year; not extended, not breaking down. Reasonable entry zone.

**Verdict.** The fundamentals alone wouldn't warrant a position, but the insider signal is strong enough to take a measured Core position ahead of a catalyst the insiders appear to be positioning for.

`INTERESTING — fits Core sleeve`

```json
{
  "ticker": "SCST.ST",

  "would_sell_at": 175.0,
  "notes_for_pm": "Earnings ~2026-07-28 (~36 days). Insiders are implicitly trading the print, so a position taken now is also a bet on that report. Size accordingly — this is a signal-driven buy, not a quality compounder. If PM passes now and the report confirms, a post-earnings entry is still likely valid."
}
```

# AAK (AAK.ST) — Analyst note

**Thesis.** AAK is a quality specialty-fats business trading ~20% off its 52-week high after a sell-side downgrade, while five insiders — including the CEO, CFO and Chairman — bought ~5.9 MSEK of stock in late April / early May right after the Q1 report. That cluster, on a high-quality consumer staples name, is the kind of setup worth taking seriously.

**Positives**
- Genuine insider cluster: 5 distinct buyers, 0 sellers, spanning CEO, CFO, Chairman, a board member and senior management — broad-based conviction, not one opportunistic buy.
- Quality fundamentals: ROE 16%, op margin 10%, net debt/equity only 10% — a defensive staples business with a clean balance sheet.
- Valuation has compressed: P/E 17 on a quality compounder, 4.1% dividend yield, –20% from 52w high and –10% vs 200-day MA — buying weakness, not strength.
- Insider buys (Apr 28 – May 8) came right after the Q1 report, so management was acting with the freshest internal numbers in hand.

**Concerns**
- Revenue growth is anemic: +0.3% YoY and –2.9% 3y CAGR. The "quality compounder" label needs an earnings driver I can't see from this data.
- Danske Bank cut the price target on June 18 citing "increased concern about the outlook" — I don't have the actual report or the specific concern (volumes? mix? a customer?), which is a real gap.
- FCF yield is only 2.4% despite the drawdown — not screaming cheap on cash terms.
- Next earnings ~July 28 (~36 days out) — any new position carries event risk into a print where the sell-side is already nervous.
- I cannot see the Q1 report itself, segment trends, or the specifics of Danske's downgrade — the insider cluster is my strongest evidence, and I'm leaning on it heavily.

**Insider read.** Strong positive signal. Five distinct buyers including all three of CEO/CFO/Chairman, no sellers, ~5.9 MSEK gross, all clustered within ~10 days of the Q1 report. On a Large Cap staples name this size of cluster is meaningful — these people see the next quarter's pipeline.

**Price read.** –11% over the past month, –13% over 12 months, –20% from 52w high, –10% vs 200-day MA. Clear downtrend, accelerated by the June 18 broker cut. Vol30d 24% is elevated for a staples name, consistent with the recent drop.

**Verdict.** The setup — quality balance sheet, multi-insider cluster post-report, meaningful drawdown, sell-side capitulation — is the textbook contrarian Core entry. The honest hesitation is the July 28 print and that I can't see what Danske is worried about. I'd want the PM to consider a *starter* position now and add after earnings, rather than a full-size buy into the event.

`INTERESTING — fits Core sleeve`

The insider cluster is the single strongest piece of evidence here; if you discount insider signal heavily, this becomes a PASS."

RISK CAPS (mirrors constitution §4 — enforced in code):
  - Max 15% single holding (of total portfolio)
  - Max 25% any single sector (of total)
  - Max 10% single Aggressive position
  - Max 20% Aggressive sleeve total
  - Min 30% cash buffer of the Core sleeve
  - Max 90% total equity exposure
  - Max ~10 holdings
  - Min 4-week holding period — early full exit requires thesis_break=True + rationale
response_text:
## Trigger read

Three things hit today: (1) media speculation of an H&M CEO change — unconfirmed, no price reaction, but partially undercuts the KJ Persson conviction signal we own HM-B for; (2) three new insider-cluster names — SECU-B (Analyst says WATCH), SCST and AAK (both INTERESTING for Core). All three new names have earnings landing in the same late-July cluster we're already overweight on.

## Decision

**HM-B — hold, no action.** The CEO rumour is Börsvärlden speculation, no confirmation, no price move. Our thesis rests on KJ Persson's 1.2 BSEK insider buying — a CEO change doesn't invalidate that; if anything the principal owner often *drives* such changes and the signal can survive it. But it's a genuine new piece of information. Right call is to reassess at the Sunday weekly cycle with the journal keeper, not exit a 4.8% starter on a rumour today. Position is also inside its 4-week min hold (opened 2026-05-24, day 29 — right at the edge, no thesis break).

**SCST and AAK — defer to Sunday.** Both are genuinely interesting clusters. Neither is time-sensitive: SCST has consolidated 3 months flat, AAK is in a steady downtrend. Nothing fires today that says "Sunday is too late." Critically, both report ~Jul 28 — which lands them in the *exact same week* as VOLV/ESSITY/ASSA/THULE. Adding two more late-July earnings exposures without the weekly cycle's full review is exactly the kind of impulse the constitution warns against. The bar for intra-week action is higher, not lower.

**SECU-B — watchlist only.** Analyst already said WATCH (Latour overhang + declining revenue). Easy pass.

## Risk awareness

Cash 66.5%, plenty of room. No caps tight. Consumer Discretionary at ~13% across HM/Thule/Rusta is the one to monitor — adds there need rebalancing. The real constraint isn't a cap, it's the July 22–29 earnings cluster: I already own five names reporting in that window and shouldn't pile on more single-handedly on a Monday.

## Conclusion

Nothing here rises above the bar. The HM-B rumour is information for Sunday's review, not an exit trigger. The two interesting new clusters can wait four days for a proper weekly assessment alongside the existing book.

```json
{
  "summary": "No action — HM-B CEO rumour is unconfirmed and not an exit trigger; SCST/AAK are interesting but not time-sensitive and belong in the weekly review.",
  "trades": [],
  "no_action_note": "HM-B media speculation is unconfirmed with no price reaction — reassess at weekly cycle, not a thesis break. SCST and AAK insider clusters are genuine but neither price action nor a near-term event makes Sunday too late; both report ~Jul 28 adding to an already-heavy late-July earnings cluster, so they deserve full weekly-cycle deliberation. SECU-B is WATCH per analyst."
}
```
